name: Panoramic Hillsides Trail
name_original: Boucle vallonnée entre Floirac et Bouliac
description: This moderate 7.2-mile jogging loop between Floirac and Bouliac offers 710 feet of elevation gain, diverse scenery, and panoramic views of B
region_intro: Jogging around Cenon offers a blend of urban green spaces and natural river landscapes in the Gironde department. The area features Palmer Park, a 25-hectare green corridor with winding paths, and the expansive Parc des Coteaux, providing panoramic views and a network of trails. Its location on the right bank of the Garonne River also provides access to scenic riverside running routes. The terrain includes gentle hillsides and varied paths suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Cenon The most popular jogging route is Panoramic Hillsides Trail, a 7.2 miles (11.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 21 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views over the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local runners is Place de la Bourse – Jacques Chaban-Delmas Bridge loop from Cenon, a difficult 13.8 miles (22.2 km) path. This route connects urban landmarks with riverside scenery. Local runners also love the Le Puit - Angelica Park – View from Pont de Pierre loop from Cenon, a 5.3 miles (8.5 km) trail leading through Angelica Park and offering views of the Pont de Pierre, often completed in about 53 minutes. Jogging around Cenon is defined by riverside paths along the Garonne, green spaces like Palmer Park, and trails through the Parc des Coteaux. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Cenon are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 2000 runners have used komoot to explore Cenon's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Cenon?
A: Cenon offers a diverse selection of over 30 running routes. These paths cater to various preferences, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging runs, ensuring there's something for every runner.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Cenon?
A: The running trails in Cenon feature a mix of urban green spaces, gentle hillsides, and scenic riverside paths along the Garonne. You'll find varied terrain, from the winding paths of Palmer Park to the more expansive trails within Parc des Coteaux, offering diverse running experiences.

Q: Are there any easy running routes suitable for beginners or a relaxed jog?
A: Yes, Cenon has several routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier pace. There are at least two routes classified as easy, and many of the moderate trails also offer accessible sections. For example, routes through Palmer Park provide a gentler experience.

Q: Can I find running routes with scenic viewpoints in Cenon?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e843127920/panoramic-hillsides-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Panoramic Hillsides Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers beautiful views over the surrounding landscape. Additionally, the Parc des Coteaux provides expansive natural settings with panoramic vistas of Bordeaux and the countryside, perfect for a scenic run.

Q: Are there any running paths along the Garonne River?
A: Yes, Cenon's location on the right bank of the Garonne River provides direct access to scenic riverside running routes. The nearby Bordeaux Waterfront, just 4 kilometers away, also features a popular riverwalk that is excellent for jogging with urban views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41012654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Place de la Bourse – Jacques Chaban-Delmas Bridge loop from Cenon&lt;/a&gt; connects urban landmarks with riverside scenery.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Cenon?
A: The running routes in Cenon are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Runners often praise the blend of urban green spaces and natural river landscapes, as well as the variety of terrain available for different running preferences.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions I can see while running in Cenon?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate interesting landmarks. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38572405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Le Puit - Angelica Park – View from Pont de Pierre loop from Cenon&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5873675" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont de Pierre (Stone Bridge)&lt;/a&gt;. Other routes might pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/929964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bordeaux Water Mirror&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4024666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain of the Three Graces&lt;/a&gt; in Place de la Bourse.

Q: Are there any running routes that are circular?
A: Many of the running routes around Cenon are designed as loops, offering circular paths that bring you back to your starting point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41012654" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Place de la Bourse – Jacques Chaban-Delmas Bridge loop from Cenon&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38572405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Le Puit - Angelica Park – View from Pont de Pierre loop from Cenon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any specific parks or green spaces ideal for running in Cenon?
A: Palmer Park is a fantastic option, a 25-hectare 'verdant oasis' in the heart of Cenon with extensive winding paths perfect for joggers. The Parc des Coteaux also offers a network of trails in a more expansive natural setting, providing an escape from the urban environment.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, Cenon offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41195171" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Floirac Church – View of Bouliac loop from Cenon&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 30.1 km path with significant elevation gain, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Cenon?
A: Cenon's climate in Nouvelle-Aquitaine generally allows for pleasant running conditions throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer mild temperatures, ideal for longer runs. Summer mornings and evenings are also great, though some routes might offer more shade than others during hotter periods.

Q: Are there options for parking near the running trails in Cenon?
A: Given Cenon's blend of urban convenience and natural beauty, many popular running spots like Palmer Park and areas near the Garonne River typically have accessible parking options. It's advisable to check specific route starting points for detailed parking information.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog the Hilly loop between Floirac and Bouliac?
faqs.answer: This moderate jogging route typically takes around 1 hour and 21 minutes to complete. However, your actual time may vary depending on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route is characterized by its undulating, hilly terrain, traversing lush valley bottoms, rich wine-growing hillsides, and upscale residential plateaus. You can expect a mix of unpaved paths, natural surfaces, and some paved sections. Be aware that during certain seasons, particularly in winter, mud can be present on the trails.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Hilly loop between Floirac and Bouliac?
faqs.answer: Starting points like the Parc du Castel in Floirac are generally accessible. While specific parking details aren't provided, areas near public parks or residential zones often offer street parking. Consider checking local maps for parking facilities near the Parc du Castel or other potential trailheads in Floirac or Bouliac.

faqs.question: Is the Hilly loop between Floirac and Bouliac dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife, near roads, or when passing through residential sections. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the trail for everyone.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery, including panoramic views of Floirac and Bordeaux, particularly from the belvedere of Bouliac church. You'll pass through wooded areas, past historical sites like the ruins of Château du Grand Dragon, and through various parks, including Parc du Castel and the expansive Parc de la Burthe. You might also spot the Cypressat Park Chapel and the Château du Dragon.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. During winter, especially around December, be prepared for potentially muddy conditions due to events like the Soli'trail de Bouliac. Summer can be warm, so an early morning or late afternoon jog is advisable.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, portions of this route are part of the GR Bordeaux Métropole, a long-distance urban hiking trail. It also intersects with sections of the Étape 7 • Chemin d'Amadour, Super Balade 7 - Balade panoramique des coteaux, and ReVE 14 - Périphérique.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this jogging route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to access and jog this loop. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty grade and significant elevation gain of over 200 meters, this specific 11.6 km loop might be challenging for absolute beginners. However, shorter, easier loops exist in the Floirac-Bouliac area. If you're new to jogging, consider starting with those or taking this route at a slower pace.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints along the Hilly loop between Floirac and Bouliac?
faqs.answer: Yes, the frequent elevation changes offer numerous vantage points. You can expect stunning panoramic views over Bordeaux and the Garonne River, particularly from the belvedere of Bouliac church. The route also provides superb landscapes of the Entre-deux-mers.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for mud, especially in cooler months, wear appropriate trail running shoes with good grip. Dress in layers to adapt to changing temperatures. Bring water, especially on warmer days, as there's no mention of water sources directly on the trail. A phone for navigation and emergencies is also recommended.
